title: Aphelandra mucronata
taxonomy: Aphelandra mucronata is classified within the plant family Acanthaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or thorny flower family. This family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Aphelandra mucronata is also classified as a member of the genus Aphelandra, a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The genus Aphelandra is further divided into several species, with Aphelandra mucronata being one of them. The classification of Aphelandra mucronata reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary history within the Acanthaceae family.

intro: Aphelandra mucronata, also known as Zebra Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, characterized by its striking zebra-striped leaves and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its reported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and antimicrobial activity.
